meta.title: Jaguar AJD4 / 204DTD Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Jaguar AJD4 / 204DTD Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Jaguar AJD4 is a 2.2-litre four-cylinder turbodiesel — a capable unit, but timing chain and EGR issues are common. Backed by 590+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [JAG-VERIFIED].

rows.value: Confidence: 88% confidence — based on 590 UK enquiries and cross-referenced specialist reports.

rows.metric: A mix of cheap fixes (EGR, injectors) and a few expensive ones (timing chain, oil dilution) — anchored to the middle anchor point

rows.value: Available but with some wait or aftermarket-only options — Jaguar specialist network is smaller than BMW/Mercedes

rows.metric: Reconditioned £7,250 (fitted) against conservative £8,000 host value = 45–59% band → 12

rows.value: Sum of the five rows above

rows.metric: One-line verdict: "The AJD4 is a capable, economical diesel — but timing chain tensioner wear and EGR issues are common — buy only with full service history." (Clause 1: 8 words. Clause 2: 9 words. Clause 3: 6 words. Total: 23 words.)
rows.value: Best for: High-mileage motorway drivers who service on schedule.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an AJD4 / 204DTD typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [JAG-QUOTE].

items.question: How much does a Jaguar AJD4 / 204DTD engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned AJD4 typically costs £3,500–£5,800 supply only [JAG-QUOTE], varying by mileage, condition, and supplier warranty. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

items.question: Is the Jaguar AJD4 / 204DTD a reliable engine?
items.answer: The AJD4 scores 3.5★ out of 5, with 88% confidence based on 590+ real UK enquiries [JAG-VERIFIED]. It's a capable motorway diesel, but timing chain tensioner wear and EGR clogging are documented weak points.

items.question: What is the most common Jaguar AJD4 / 204DTD failure?
items.answer: Timing chain tensioner wear is the most reported failure — it can stretch and cause rattling on cold start, typically appearing between 70,000–120,000 miles. EGR cooler leaks and oil dilution from DPF regeneration are also common.

items.question: Which Jaguar models use the AJD4 / 204DTD?
items.answer: The AJD4 / 204DTD was fitted to the XF (X250), XJ (X351), and early examples of the XE (X760) and F-Pace (X761) — primarily 2011–2015 production.

items.question: How long does the Jaguar AJD4 / 204DTD last?
items.answer: With regular servicing and timely timing-chain maintenance, the AJD4 / 204DTD can reach 150,000–180,000 miles. Short urban trips accelerate DPF and EGR issues — motorway usage extends engine life significantly.
